user_message: Give me the manager view on this Tailspin Toys exec meeting notes. The customer escalated this morning.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-04 10:21 EST
Subject: Re: migration architecture Discussion - Next Steps

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with migration architecture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
Date: 2024-02-05 17:43 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Tailspin Toys migration architecture

Just checking in on the migration architecture proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-28 13:30 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Tailspin Toys migration architecture

There's been a change in our timeline for migration architecture. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Tailspin Toys: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF37-L trap (No native support-handoff routing in the skill set); produces complete Severity triage, root-cause hypothesis (labeled as hypothesis), customer response draft, ACR risk fl
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF37-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF37-L failure mode (No native support-handoff routing in the skill set)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF37-L -- No native support-handoff routing in the skill set
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
